title: Hightouch Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Hightouch** is a Reverse-ETL / Composable Customer Data Platform that activates data from the warehouse into hundreds of SaaS destinations. It exposes a single REST surface rooted at `https://api.hightouch.com/api/v1`. This connector — `HightouchConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "hightouch"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Workspaces | `/workspaces` | List workspaces the API token can see |
| Sources | `/sources` | List + read warehouse / DB sources |
| Models | `/models` | List + read SQL / audience definitions |
| Destinations | `/destinations` | List + read SaaS destinations |
| Syncs | `/syncs` | List + read + create + trigger reverse-ETL pipelines |
| Sync Runs | `/syncs/{id}/runs` | List + read individual run history |
| Sequences | `/sequences` | List orchestrated multi-step sync sequences |
| Events | `/events` | Forward Customer Studio events into Hightouch |

The connector normalises Sources + Models + Destinations + Syncs into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), exposes one standalone `async def` per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429 / 5xx with exponential backoff + jitter (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::HightouchHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Mint a Hightouch API token

1. Sign in to https://app.hightouch.com.
2. Open **Settings → API Keys** (workspace-scoped) **OR** **Settings → Personal Access Tokens** (user-scoped).
3. Click **Create API Key**, name it `shielva-acp`, grant Read+Write scopes for the resource families you'll use (Sources, Models, Destinations, Syncs).
4. Copy the token value — Hightouch shows it only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Hightouch**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_token` (secret) | Token from step 4 |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.hightouch.com/api/v1` (rare — sandbox/regional only) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_token` is non-empty (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `HightouchHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /workspaces` as a lightweight, idempotent reachability check. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 5xx → `OFFLINE + CONNECTED` with a structured warning log.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Hightouch REST API uses **server-to-server bearer token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). There is no OAuth dance, no token refresh, and no expiry — the token is valid until manually revoked in the Hightouch dashboard.

### Credential model

- `api_token` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <api_token>`.
- The legacy field name `api_key` is still accepted at install time for back-compat (see `connector.py::__init__`) but new installs must use `api_token`.
- No `wix-account-id` / `x-workspace-id` style fan-out headers — the token itself scopes the request to a workspace.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_token`. Initialises `HightouchHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Hightouch endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured `api_token` (no token exchange). |
| `health_check()` | `GET /workspaces` — minimal probe, retried twice on transient failure. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None — the api_token lives in `self.config` and is encrypted at rest by the gateway credential vault. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request to `https://api.hightouch.com/api/v1/*`:

```
Authorization: Bearer <api_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

A call attempted with an empty `api_token` raises `HightouchAuthError` from the HTTP layer immediately — the connector never silently sends an unauthenticated request.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_token` | secret | yes | `self.api_token` | `Authorization: Bearer …` value |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.hightouch.com/api/v1`) | Override for Hightouch sandbox or regional endpoint |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap; Hightouch enforces its own server-side limit |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_HIGHTOUCH_BASE = "https://api.hightouch.com/api/v1"
```

### Retry tuning (single-owner in `client/http_client.py`)

```python
RETRY_DELAY_S       = 1.0   # first retry after 1 s
BACKOFF_FACTOR      = 2.0   # exponential
MAX_RETRY_DELAY_S   = 32.0  # cap so a long outage doesn't stall forever
DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES = 3
DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S   = 30.0  # httpx per-request budget
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `HightouchConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::HightouchHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_token` is non-empty; initialise `HightouchHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API. Returns `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` when `api_token` is empty; `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)` on success.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a `TokenInfo(access_token=self.api_token, token_type="api_key")` with empty refresh + expiry.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /workspaces` (retried up to 2 times) — minimal reachability probe. Classifies failures via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Hightouch inventory (sources → models → destinations → syncs) → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_found / _synced / _failed` counters. Hightouch is reverse-ETL — there is no row corpus to backfill, so the sync mirrors the workspace inventory for KB introspection.

children.title: Workspaces
children.content:
### `async list_workspaces() -> Dict`
`GET /workspaces` — list every workspace the api_token can see. Used as the health-check probe.

children.title: Sources
children.content:
### `async list_sources(page=1, per_page=50, slug=None) -> Dict`
`GET /sources?page={N}&per_page={M}&slug={slug}` — list configured warehouse / DB sources. `slug` is an exact-match filter (omit for all).

### `async get_source(source_id) -> Dict`
`GET /sources/{id}` — fetch a single source by numeric id. Coerces id to `str` for path construction.

children.title: Models
children.content:
### `async list_models(page=1, per_page=50, slug=None) -> Dict`
`GET /models` — list SQL / audience model definitions.

### `async get_model(model_id) -> Dict`
`GET /models/{id}`.

children.title: Destinations
children.content:
### `async list_destinations(page=1, per_page=50, slug=None) -> Dict`
`GET /destinations` — list configured SaaS destinations.

### `async get_destination(destination_id) -> Dict`
`GET /destinations/{id}`.

children.title: Syncs
children.content:
### `async list_syncs(page=1, per_page=50, slug=None, model_id=None, destination_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /syncs?modelId={mid}&destinationId={did}` — list sync pipelines. Filters mirror Hightouch's camelCase wire format.

### `async get_sync(sync_id) -> Dict`
`GET /syncs/{id}`.

### `async create_sync(payload: Dict) -> Dict`
`POST /syncs` — body is the raw sync definition (`slug`, `modelId`, `destinationId`, `configuration`, `schedule`). Hightouch validates server-side.

### `async run_sync(sync_id, full_resync=False) -> Dict`
`POST /syncs/{id}/trigger` — body `{"fullResync": bool}`. Returns `{"syncRunId": <int>}` on accept.

### `async list_sync_runs(sync_id, page=1, per_page=50) -> Dict`
`GET /syncs/{id}/runs` — paginated run history.

### `async get_sync_run(sync_id, run_id) -> Dict`
`GET /syncs/{sid}/runs/{rid}`.

children.title: Sequences + Events
children.content:
### `async list_sequences(page=1, per_page=50) -> Dict`
`GET /sequences` — orchestrated multi-step sync sequences.

### `async send_event(event: Dict) -> Dict`
`POST /events` — forward a Customer Studio event envelope (`userId`, `anonymousId`, `event`, `properties`, `timestamp`). Useful when Shielva acts as the event collector for downstream Hightouch activation.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
HightouchError                         # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── HightouchAuthError                 # 401 / 403
├── HightouchBadRequestError           # 400
├── HightouchNotFoundError             # 404 — also carries resource_id
├── HightouchConflictError             # 409 (duplicate slug)
├── HightouchRateLimitError            # 429 — retry_after_s
└── HightouchServerError               # 5xx (and transport-layer errors)
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
HightouchNetworkError = HightouchServerError
HightouchNotFound     = HightouchNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately — no retry |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `RETRY_DELAY_S * BACKOFF_FACTOR ** attempt + jitter` (1s, 2s, 4s …) capped at `MAX_RETRY_DELAY_S=32s`, up to `DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES=3` attempts |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff + jitter, up to 3 retries |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `HightouchServerError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)         # via HightouchAuthError
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)             # after exhausting retries
5xx → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   CONNECTED)             # via HightouchServerError
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_token` blank in install fields | Re-enter the token in ACP. Hightouch shows the value only once at creation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — token revoked in Hightouch dashboard or wrong workspace | Confirm the token still exists at **Hightouch → Settings → API Keys**; rotate and re-install if missing. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` despite token existing | 403 — token lacks required scopes | Edit the API key in Hightouch and grant Read+Write for the resource families the connector uses (Sources, Models, Destinations, Syncs, Sequences, Events). |
| `hightouch.http.retry` warnings spike, then succeeds | Hitting 429 (per-token rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff + jitter. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` install field or shrink sync batch size. |
| 404 on `get_source(123)` / `get_sync(33)` | Resource id from a different workspace than the api_token's workspace | API tokens are workspace-scoped. Confirm the resource id belongs to the same workspace the token was minted in. |
| `HightouchAuthError: api_token is required for Hightouch API calls` raised on first method call | Connector instantiated with empty `api_token` AND `api_key` config | Ensure install fields populated. The HTTP layer fails-closed — it will not send an unauthenticated request. |
| `create_sync(payload)` returns 400 with `"slug already exists"` | Duplicate slug in the workspace | Choose a unique slug or fetch the existing sync via `list_syncs(slug=…)` and `update`. |
| `run_sync(33, full_resync=True)` accepted but no rows activated | Sync is disabled (`disabled: true`) in Hightouch | Re-enable the sync in the Hightouch dashboard before triggering. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'HightouchNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `HightouchNetworkError = HightouchServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
